source: Process failure observed this session — repeated 'verify PASS' reported while verify actually FAILed.
title: verify.py true exit code lost when output is piped (| tail) → false PASS
what_failed: Running 'python scripts/verify.py | tail -8' reports tail's exit code (always 0), masking verify's exit 1. Multiple 'PASS' claims this session were actually FAIL until the real exit code was read with '; echo $?'.
where_failed: Any piped invocation of verify.py in a shell.
why_likely: Shell pipelines return the last command's status unless pipefail is set; Git Bash default has no pipefail.
migration_plan: 1. Document: never read verify.py through a pipe; use '; echo EXIT=$?' or redirect to a file.
2. Optional: a thin wrapper script verify that prints a bold PASS/FAIL banner and 'exit $code', and/or a note in CLAUDE.md.
3. Optional: have verify.py write a machine-readable status sentinel (already writes docs/STATUS.md Overall: FAIL).
analysis: Cheap to mitigate; the real cost was trusting a piped PASS. STATUS.md already records the truthful overall result — read that, not the piped stdout.
resolution: Added T-214 warning block to CLAUDE.md Commands section: 'NEVER pipe verify.py; use python scripts/verify.py --quiet; echo EXIT=$?'. docs/STATUS.md is the authoritative result file.
